title: Security Onion on Proxmox
snippet: Security Onion on ProxmoxI originally set up my homelab using Ovirt, but have since switched back to Proxmox. The reason for that is that the version of qemu that Ovirt ships with does not support the “applesmc” device that is needed to run macOS guests, whereas Proxmox does. Another benefit is t...

title: Hacking a Computer Remotely through a Phone
snippet: In a recent demonstration of cyber and electronic warfare capabilities, I had the opportunity to enable access into a network by exploiting a computer remotely through a cell phone. title: Installing the Cuckoo Sandbox Using KVM
snippet: The Cuckoo project provides a safe environment in which to execute malware (also called “detonating”). I will be using Cuckoo as part of a malware analysis class. There are several guides that you could follow to setup Cuckoo, but almost all of the ones that I found used VirtualBox as a hyperviso...

title: Investigating DoH
snippet: DNS SecurityAs a plain-text protocol, DNS lacks Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA) protections. An attacker who can observe DNS activity can see where the DNS request originated from, where responses came from, what the query and response were, or tamper with the response.DNS over...
